body:
For example, when i want to launch a set of tasks (from existing issues) and do not want to create a new dedicated parent issue, I kinda want to 
- create a comment in a discussion (like https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/80 ) then tag the orchestrate agent 
- then the orchestrate agent can work on it, and then comment inside the thread. 
    - a thread in the discussion is kinda acting as an "issue" now and share its own context for managing the tasks and handoffs 


@sepo-agent /answer what do you think?

comments.body:
I think this is the right product shape, with one important design constraint: the discussion thread should become the **coordination surface**, but execution should still reuse the existing issue/PR-backed action workflows.

Current state:
- Discussion mentions and replies are already recognized trigger surfaces: [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md#L136-L158).
- Responses can already be posted back into a discussion comment thread via `replyToId`: [`respond.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/respond.ts#L56-L64).
- But `/orchestrate` is currently gated to only issues and PRs: [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml#L655-L662).
- The orchestrator workflow itself also models `target_number` / `target_kind` as issue-or-PR state: [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml#L16-L22).
- Existing sub-orchestration is intentionally issue-backed: child state markers, parent progress comments, and parent resume all assume an issue parent: [`agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L73-L103), [`sub-orchestration.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/sub-orchestration.ts#L117-L146).

So I would not make the discussion comment literally masquerade as an issue inside the existing issue-only code. I would add a thin first-class **discussion-thread orchestration parent** layer:

1. Treat a discussion comment/reply mention as an orchestration parent target keyed by:
   - discussion number
   - discussion node id
   - anchor comment/reply node id
   - requester/auth context
2. Let the planner run against that thread transcript as the parent context.
3. For each concrete task, reuse the existing `delegate_issue` path to create/adopt child issues.
4. Report child completion back into the original discussion thread, using the existing threaded discussion reply support.
5. Resume the discussion-thread parent orchestrator from that report, just like issue parent orchestration resumes today.
6. Store hidden deterministic markers in agent-authored discussion replies, not user-authored discussion text.

That preserves the UX you want: “this discussion thread acts like the parent issue,” while keeping implementation/review/fix-pr workflows anchored to normal GitHub issues and PRs where labels, closing refs, branches, and PR review state already work.

Execution plan I’d propose:

1. Extend router orchestration gating to allow `target_kind == discussion` when the source is `discussion_comment`.
2. Add a discussion-thread parent identity, probably not just `discussion#80`, because multiple comments inside the same discussion need separate orchestration state.
3. Add GraphQL helpers for fetching a focused discussion comment thread transcript and for updating/creating trusted agent marker replies.
4. Extend orchestrator preflight/dispatch to accept `discussion_thread` parent targets for planner starts only.
5. Keep `delegate_issue` children as normal issues, but allow their terminal report path to post back to either an issue parent or a discussion-thread parent.
6. Update docs/tests around supported workflows, request lifecycle, orchestrator details, and discussion reply behavior.

I’d be comfortable implementing this behind the existing explicit `/orchestrate` route. Approval question: should the first version support only **top-level discussion comments as orchestration parents**, or should replies nested under a discussion comment also be valid separate parent threads?

@sepo-agent /answer 

This might be too complicated: 

1. What I think: besides the existing functions (issue/PR based orchestration), we can just add a way to coordinate multiple issues in a thread inside a discussion page (like https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/80 ) 
2. let's say we can create a comment on a series of tasks to do(without creating a new redundant issue), and the agent orchestrator can use the reply threads as a way to coordinate the work there. 
3. we do not need to remove existing functions, but just add this smaller layer of discussion based management.

Yes, I agree with this narrower version. It is much cleaner than making discussions a full replacement for issue/PR orchestration.

The split I’d use:

- Existing issue/PR orchestration stays unchanged.
- A discussion reply thread becomes a lightweight **coordination surface**.
- The actual tasks remain existing issues/PRs.
- The agent posts progress, handoffs, blockers, and final summaries back into that discussion thread.

That fits the current architecture well because discussion mentions are already supported as trigger surfaces in [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md#L136-L158), and threaded discussion replies are already supported in [`respond.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/respond.ts#L56-L121). The main missing piece is that `/orchestrate` currently only dispatches for issue or PR targets in [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml#L655-L662).

I would implement this as a small “discussion coordinator” layer:

1. User writes a discussion comment like:
   ```md
   @sepo-agent /orchestrate
   Coordinate #101, #102, #103. Do them in this order...
   ```
2. Router allows `/orchestrate` from `discussion_comment`, but marks it as `discussion coordination`, not normal issue orchestration.
3. The coordinator parses/derives the issue list from the thread.
4. For each issue, it dispatches the existing issue orchestration path.
5. It stores a hidden marker in an agent-authored discussion reply with the coordinated issue list and current state.
6. Child issue completions report back to the same discussion reply thread.
7. The discussion thread becomes the human-readable “control room,” without creating a redundant parent issue.

This avoids changing the core invariant documented in [`agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L73-L103): implementation/review/fix-pr work still happens through issue/PR targets. We only add a lightweight discussion-level manager above it.

The first version can be modest:

- only explicit `/orchestrate` in discussion comments
- only existing issue numbers, no automatic task creation
- post progress into the same discussion reply thread
- no labels/milestones/project metadata
- no replacement for parent issue orchestration

I think that’s the right scope. It gives you the workflow you want without overloading Discussions with all the semantics of Issues.

title: (sub)-Orchestration inside discussion threads
